content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 7, 1989 
Bob Connelly 
Chief Administrative Officer 
Assembly Rules Committee 
state Capitol, Room 3016 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Dear Mr. Connelly: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-306 
You have requested advice regarding the mass mailing provi-
sions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).1 This letter will 
confirm telephone advice previously provided. 
QUESTION 
Does the town hall meeting notice that you have submitted on 
behalf of Assemblyman Lempert constitute an exception to the mass 
mailing prohibition as a notice of public meeting and, therefore, 
a permissible mass mailing? 
CONCLUSION 
The notice does not fall within the exception to the mass 
mailing prohibition and cannot be sent at public expense for the 
following reasons: 
1. 
The Assemblyman's name may only appear once in the return 
address portion of the "envelope" portion of the self-mailer. It 
may not appear a second time in the town hall box. 
2. 
The Assemblyman's name may only appear once on the notice 
itself--either designating who is calling the meeting or in a 
1 
~ 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Section 
18000, ~~. All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322 .. 5660 


OUr Pile No. A-89-l06 
Page 2 
concise description of the subject-matter of the meeting. It may 
not appear twice as it does in this notice. 
l. 
A concise description of the subject matter of the meet-
ing may not include a description of the elected official. 
4. 
The notice may not contain the phrase "Por more informa-
tion contact ••• " and the telephone number, because this informa-
tion is not permitted under the notice exception. 2 
FACTS 
Assemblyman Lempert would like to send a notice of town meet-
ing to several thousand constituents in his district. 
You have 
enclosed a copy of the proposed notice for review to see if it is 
a permissible mailing under exceptions to the mass mailing 
restrictions. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 89001 provides that no mass mailing shall be sent at 
public expense. 
A mass mailing means over 200 substantially 
similar pieces of mail unless sent in response to an unsolicited 
request, letter or other inquiry. 
(Section 82041.5.) 
Regulation 18901 clarifies that the purpose of section 89001 
is to prevent elected officers from using public funds to send out 
newsletters and other mass mailings. 
Regulation 18901 also 
provides several exceptions to the general prohibition regarding 
mass mailings. 
However, all exceptions are to be construed nar-
rowly. 
(Stirling Advice Letter, No. A-89-1l2, copy enclosed.) 
The town meeting notice that you submitted is intended to 
comply with an exception to the mass mailing requirements relating 
to notices of constituent meetings. 
Specifically, Regulation 
18901(f) (8) provides as follows: 
(f) 
The following newsletters or other mass 
mailings are not prohibited by Government Code 
Section 89001 if the mailing is sent to the persons 
specified in each instance below and the mailing 
consists of: 
* * * 
(8) 
Mailings sent to the elected officer's 
constituents which directly relate to that elected 
official's incumbent governmental duties and which 
solely include the time, date, place. and a concise 
The revised notice you have submitted in response to our 
~ telephone advice ("Notice 2", copy enclosed) 
-complies with Regulation 18901. 


Our File No. A-89-306 
Page 3 
description of the subject matter of a public 
meeting to be held by the elected officer .•. 
(Emphasis added.) 
The purpose of this exception is to allow elected officers to 
notify their constituents of public meetings to be held by the 
elected officers. 
The notice may only state the time, date, place 
and give a concise description of the subject matter of the meet-
ing. 
The notice may also contain the name of the elected officer 
once. 
(Stirling Advice Letter, supra; Murphy Advice Letter, No. 
A-89-088, copy enclosed. 
The notice that you have provided contains Assemblyman 
Lempert's name twice--once as inviting constituents to attend and 
once in the description of the subject matter. 
One of these 
references would have to be deleted. 
The reference to the as-
semblyman as "author of the Political Reform Act of 1989" is 
extraneous material describing the author rather than a concise 
description of the subject matter. 
Finally, we have previously 
advised that the portion of the notice stating "For more informa-
tion contact ... " and listing a telephone number appears to be 
outside the permissible information and therefore not permitted by 
the exception. 
(Connelly Advice Letter, No. 1-89-110, III and 
112, copy enclosed.)3 
In addition to the notice portion of the proposed mailing, 
there is also a problem with the information included in the 
"envelope" portion of the self-mailer. 
We have previously advised 
that the name of an elected officer may appear ~ 
in the return 
address portion of a self-mailer that is a standard form. 
(Mott-
Smith Advice Letter, No. A-89-l53, copy enclosed.) 
Your form 
contains a second reference to Assemblyman Lempert in the "town 
hall" box. 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please contact 
me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED/MWE:aa 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Margaret W. 
1 
Counsel, Legal Division 
The use of "for more information" may be clarified at the 
. Commission's July 12th meeting. 


Ms. Kathryn Donovan 
General Counsel 
F.P.P.C. 
May 18, 1989 
428 J street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Dear MS. Donovan: 
Enclosed is a notice of a town hall meeting that Assemblyman 
Lempert would like to send to several thousand constituents 
in his district. 
I am requesting advice as to whether this is a public 
meeting notice under the mass mailing restrictions of 
Proposition 73 and therefore, a permissible mailing. 
Sincerely, 
~4 
BOB CONNELLY 
Chief Administrative Officer

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
May 23, 1989 
Bob Connelly 
Chief Administrative Assistant 
Assembly Rules Committee 
state Capitol, Room 3016 
P.O. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-306 
Dear Mr. Connelly: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on May 22, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Margaret Ellison an attorney in the Legal 
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.
